title: thebasics:thebasics-handbook-title-tpa
text: TPA is request-based teleporting. The target player must accept before anyone moves. Server config controls whether TPA is enabled, what privilege is required, request timeout, cooldowns, and whether sending a request consumes a temporal gear. TPA commands - /tpa <player>: ask to teleport to another player. - /tpahere <player>: ask another player to teleport to you. - /tpaccept [player]: accept an incoming request. The player name is optional when only one request exists. - /tpdeny [player]: deny an incoming request. - /tpalist: list incoming requests and remaining time when timeouts are enabled. - /tpallow on|off: allow or block receiving requests. - /tpacancel: cancel your outgoing request. - /cleartpa: clear all incoming requests to you. Temporal gear behavior When the temporal gear requirement is enabled, the requester must hold a temporal gear in either hand. The gear is consumed after the request passes validation. If the request is denied, cleared, cancelled, or times out, the gear is returned to inventory, hand, or dropped at the requester's feet if there is no room.
